abstract.text: Run self-hosted macOS GitHub Actions runners in Spooktacular VMs with ephemeral isolation and automatic scaling.

inlineContent.text: Spooktacular turns every Apple Silicon Mac into a GitHub Actions
Each runner runs inside an isolated macOS VM that you can destroy
and recreate between jobs for a clean-room guarantee.
Apple Silicon supports a maximum of 2 concurrent VMs per
host. Plan your runner pools around this limit. See
Spooktacular gives you the cost savings of self-hosted infrastructure
with the clean-environment guarantees of GitHub-hosted runners.

content.text: GitHub App vs PAT for Runner Registration

inlineContent.text: Simpler to set up, but less secure for organizations:
Use fine-grained PATs with only the “Administration” permission.
More secure — the app generates short-lived tokens:

inlineContent.text: Runner fails to register or deregisters unexpectedly.
The GitHub PAT or registration token has expired.

code: # For PATs: generate a new token in GitHub Settings > Developer settings

inlineContent.text: Builds fail with “No space left on device” inside
The VM’s disk image has reached its configured maximum,

code: # Check disk usage inside the VM
# For ephemeral runners, this is self-correcting --- the VM is
# destroyed after each job. If using persistent runners:
spook exec runner-01 -- xcrun simctl delete unavailable
# Or increase disk size for new VMs

abstract.text: Creates copy-on-write clones of virtual machine bundles.
Run two isolated OpenClaw AI agent instances on a single Mac — each with its own workspace, channels, and API keys.
